The word 'งบ' (ngop) has a high tone, not a low tone.
There is a mismatch between the Thai text 'งาน' (ngaan, mid tone) and the romanization/gloss 'ying' (falling tone, meaning 'the more'). The tone label matches the romanization but not the Thai text.
The word 'จริง' (jing) has a mid-class initial consonant and a sonorant ending, which results in a mid tone, not a high tone.
